solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public String answerString(String word, int numFriends) {
        if (numFriends >= word.length()) {
            return String.valueOf(word.charAt(0));
        }

        StringBuilder result = new StringBuilder();
        for (int i = 0; i < word.length() - numFriends + 1; i++) {
            result.append(word.charAt(i));
        }

        return result.toString();
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The problem requires finding the lexicographically largest string that can be obtained from any split in any round. The key insight is that to maximize the lexicographical order, we should take the first `word.length() - numFriends + 1` characters, as splitting further would only yield smaller strings. This approach is efficient and avoids generating all possible splits.
